title: pkg/api/grpcpb: fix lint errors in generated protobuf files
body:
Fix gofumpt and inamedparam errors on macOS CI.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests
-->

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [ ] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [ ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [ ] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [ ] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [ ] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->


comments.body: I'm fine to close this and leave it to someone with access to a mac. cc @ashley-cui @l0rd as well.
To be clear doing this formatting could be correct fix, just saying we should understand the exact cause.

```
level=warning msg="[runner] Can't process results by generated_file_filter processor: can't filter issue &result.Issue{FromLinter:\"staticcheck\", Text:\"ST1000: at least one file in a package should have a package comment\", Severity:\"\", SourceLines:[]string(nil), Pkg:(*packages.Package)(0x4d4dc1bde000), Pos:token.Position{Filename:\"/Users/MacM1-4-worker/ci/task-6202887254048768/pkg/systemd/define/const.go\", Offset:0, Line:1, Column:1}, LineRange:(*result.Range)(nil), HunkPos:0, SuggestedFixes:[]analysis.SuggestedFix(nil), ExpectNoLint:false, ExpectedNoLintLinter:\"\", WorkingDirectoryRelativePath:\"../task-6202887254048768/pkg/systemd/define/const.go\", RelativePath:\"../task-6202887254048768/pkg/systemd/define/const.go\"}: failed to get doc (strict) of file /Users/MacM1-4-worker/ci/task-6202887254048768/pkg/systemd/define/const.go: failed to parse file: open /Users/MacM1-4-worker/ci/task-6202887254048768/pkg/systemd/define/const.go: no such file or directory"
```

The warning seems suspicious and AFAICT shows different "missing" files in the different test runs.

> To be clear doing this formatting could be correct fix, just saying we should understand the exact cause.

ack, i'll reopen if a better fix isn't available. LMK whenever. ..

https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6413402123272192

This shows rerunning fixed it so it does flake right now, but also the tests did run on different macos runners so it could still be related to the underlying host.

> ```
> // Code generated by protoc-gen-go. DO NOT EDIT.
> ```
> 
> I am not sure on the history but are autogenerated files supposed to be formatted like that I guess? cc @nalind

The current versions of the files are what I get when I re-run `build.sh` from that directory to regenerate them, and the commit history shows them being last generated as part of the module path rename in #28566.  I don't know that I'd expect generated code to be up to gofumpt's standards, but we could have update the script to run 'golangci-lint fmt' or 'gofumpt' on the output if it would help.
Hmm, seems like Reopen PR doesn't work. Created #28665
you cannot reopen after a new push AFAIK. I think you need to open before the push, but yeah a new PR also works just as well.
